---
title: Supabase
description: A self-hosted Supabase stack — Postgres, Auth, REST, Realtime and Storage behind Kong, with Studio. Plus how to add your own Edge Functions via an image.
---

import { Aside, CardGrid, Card } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

This template brings up a faithful self-hosted [Supabase](https://supabase.com) stack — Postgres with
Auth, REST, Realtime and Storage, all behind a **Kong** API gateway, with the **Studio** dashboard. One
click instantiates eight wired-up services. (Edge Functions are **opt-in** — you add your own image; see
below.)

## What you get

| Service          | Image                    | Role                                                            |
| ---------------- | ------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `kong` (primary) | `kong`                   | API gateway and the single public entrypoint (gets the domain). |
| `db`             | `supabase/postgres`      | Postgres with the Supabase roles, extensions and init SQL.      |
| `auth`           | `supabase/gotrue`        | Authentication (GoTrue).                                        |
| `rest`           | `postgrest/postgrest`    | Auto-generated REST API over your schema.                       |
| `realtime`       | `supabase/realtime`      | Realtime subscriptions over websockets.                         |
| `storage`        | `supabase/storage-api`   | Object storage (file backend).                                  |
| `meta`           | `supabase/postgres-meta` | Powers Studio's database browser.                               |
| `studio`         | `supabase/studio`        | The dashboard.                                                  |

## Inputs

- **`domain`** (required) — the public domain the stack is served on, e.g. `supabase.example.com`. Kong
  gets the default route here, and the public URLs (`API_EXTERNAL_URL`, `GOTRUE_SITE_URL`, Studio's
  public URL) are built from it.
- **`organization_name`** / **`project_name`** — display names shown in Studio (defaults provided).

## Generated secrets

You don't type any passwords. The template generates them per instance and wires them across the
services:

- `postgres_password`, `dashboard_password`, `secret_key_base`, `secret_key_base2`, `pg_meta_crypto_key` — random values.
- `jwt_secret` — the shared signing key.
- `anon_key` / `service_role_key` — **HS256 JWTs** signed from `jwt_secret` at instantiation, carrying
  the `anon` / `service_role` roles. These are your Supabase API keys.

## Accessing it

Open **`https://<domain>`** for Studio. It's behind HTTP basic-auth — user **`supabase`**, password = the
generated `dashboard_password`.

The API is reachable through Kong on the same domain:

- REST → `https://<domain>/rest/v1/`
- Auth → `https://<domain>/auth/v1/`
- Realtime → `wss://<domain>/realtime/v1/`
- Storage → `https://<domain>/storage/v1/`

Pass the `anon` key (public) or `service_role` key (admin) as both an `apikey` header and a
`Bearer` token.

<Aside type="caution">
	Use **`https://`**, not `http://`. The Realtime client connects over `wss://` (a TLS websocket); on a plain-HTTP endpoint the dashboard's realtime
	features fail with a `transport failure`. The realtime service itself is fine — it's the browser↔TLS path that needs HTTPS.
</Aside>

## Edge Functions (bring your own)

Edge Functions are **not part of the template** — the platform can't push function _code_ into a running
pod, so there's no useful stock runtime to ship. Instead you deploy functions by **baking them into an
image** and running that as another service, which fits how kubwave deploys everything else.

### 1. Build an image with your functions

```dockerfile
FROM supabase/edge-runtime:v1.74.0
# Your functions, including a `main` router at /home/deno/functions/main/index.ts.
COPY ./functions /home/deno/functions
```

Your `functions/` folder needs a `main/index.ts` router (use Supabase's standard one) plus each function
in its own sibling folder — `functions/hello/index.ts`, `functions/webhook/index.ts`, and so on.

### 2. Ship the image — neither way needs cluster access

<CardGrid>
	<Card title="Let kubwave build it" icon="github">
		Put the Dockerfile + `functions/` in a Git repo and create a **public repo** (or private repo) service with the **dockerfile** builder. Turn on
		**auto-deploy** and every `git push` rebuilds and rolls your functions — no external CI.
	</Card>
	<Card title="Build in GitHub Actions" icon="seti:docker">
		Build and push the image in CI, then create a **container image** service that points at it. CI only pushes an image the platform pulls — it never
		touches the cluster.
	</Card>
</CardGrid>

A GitHub Actions build looks like:

```yaml
name: deploy-functions
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: docker/login-action@v3
        with:
          registry: <your-registry>
          username: ${{ secrets.REGISTRY_USER }}
          password: ${{ secrets.REGISTRY_TOKEN }}
      - uses: docker/build-push-action@v6
        with:
          push: true
          tags: <your-registry>/supabase-functions:${{ github.sha }}
```

### 3. Add it as a service and wire it in

Create the service in the same environment as your Supabase stack:

- **Port** — `9000`.
- **Arguments** — `start`, then `--main-service`, then `/home/deno/functions/main` (one token per row,
  under the service's **Command & Arguments** settings). This is the edge-runtime entrypoint; without it
  the container exits with `exec: "start": not found`.
- **Environment** — point it at the stack: `SUPABASE_URL` = the internal Kong host
  (`http://<kong-service-host>:8000`), plus `JWT_SECRET`, `SUPABASE_ANON_KEY`,
  `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` and `SUPABASE_DB_URL` set to the same generated values the other Supabase
  services use.

### 4. Route it through Kong

Edit the **`kong`** service's `kong.yml` config file (under its **Config files** settings) and add a
route to your functions service's internal host:

```yaml
- name: functions-v1
  url: 'http://<your-functions-service-host>:9000/'
  routes:
    - name: functions-v1
      strip_path: true
      paths: ['/functions/v1/']
  plugins:
    - name: cors
```

Then call your functions through the gateway:

```
POST https://<domain>/functions/v1/hello
Authorization: Bearer <anon-or-service_role-key>
apikey: <anon-or-service_role-key>
```

<Aside type="note">
	**Don't use `scp` + restart from the Supabase docs.** That method assumes a docker-compose host with a bind-mounted `volumes/functions` folder and
	SSH access — neither exists on Kubernetes. The image route above is the supported way here.
</Aside>

<Aside type="caution">
	Studio's **Functions** tab is a hosted-platform feature for managing functions from the dashboard; on self-hosted Supabase it can't list or deploy
	functions and may error. Manage functions through your image/repo as above.
</Aside>

## Not included

- **Logs / Analytics** (Logflare + Vector) is not part of this template: Vector needs host-level access
  to the node's pod logs (a `hostPath` mount), which kubwave's sandboxed tenant model doesn't grant. The
  Studio **Logs** tab stays empty.
